title: Faulty Rotated Binary Search with Duplicate Minima

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat:
Input format: 

int n, target
vector<int> nums
statement.outputFormat: Output format: bool, indicating whether target is present in the array
statement.notes: Time and space complexity expectations: O(log n), O(1)

examples.explanation: Target 2 is not present in the array
Target 3 is present in the array

hints.text: The bug in the given code is due to incorrect handling of duplicate minima in the rotated sorted array.

editorial.title: Editorial for Rotated Binary Search
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem lies in correctly identifying the first occurrence of the target element in the rotated sorted array.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can find the first occurrence of the target by performing a binary search on the array. To do this, we must correctly handle the case where the target is the duplicate minima. We can use the fact that the array is sorted to our advantage.
